patch.value: Extension install: untrusted manifest dependency specs reach npm install unvalidated (arg injection all-OS; shell command injection on Windows)

installNpmPackageRuntimeDependencies() reads the package.json of a freshly npm-pack'd third-party extension (fully attacker-controlled) and builds install specs as `${name}@${version.trim()}` from arbitrary object keys/values in dependencies/optionalDependencies/peerDependencies (runtimeDependencyInstallSpecs, ~L767). These specs are passed to runNpmCommand -> execFile('npm', ['install','--ignore-scripts',...,...specs]). No validation exists: line 409's guard is only on the install-SOURCE spec, not on manifest dependency names/versions.

Two vectors, both requiring only that a user runs 'pm install <malicious-extension>':
1. Argument injection (ALL platforms): a dependency KEY beginning with '-'/'--' (e.g. {"--foo":"1"} -> spec `--foo@1`) is appended after --ignore-scripts and parsed by npm's lenient nopt as an option, letting a manifest inject npm flags into a privileged install and probe for a way to re-enable lifecycle scripts / redirect --registry.
2. Command injection (WINDOWS): shouldRunNpmCommandInShell() returns true on win32, so execFile runs with shell:true and concatenates argv into a shell command line. An unquoted version value such as '1.0.0 & <cmd>' yields shell command execution during pm install.

This defeats the deliberate --ignore-scripts hardening the authors added precisely because extension installs are untrusted. Fix = validate names/versions against npm grammar, reject leading-dash names and metachar versions, and insert a '--' end-of-options separator before the spec list (mirrors the passthrough contract pattern shipped in pm-albl). Class-relate to the spawn-shell-true Semgrep batch (pm-ngjd) but note this one is untrusted-INPUT-driven, not a static command string.

runtimeDependencyInstallSpecs (src/cli/commands/extension/install-sources.ts) validates each dependency name against the npm package-name grammar and rejects/skips any name beginning with '-' so a manifest key cannot become an npm CLI flag; version values are validated against the npm version/range/dist-tag/url grammar and rejected if they contain shell metacharacters; a '--' end-of-options separator precedes the spec list passed to npm install so no spec can be parsed as a flag on any platform; regression specs cover a leading-dash dependency name, a version containing shell metacharacters, and confirm --ignore-scripts cannot be overridden by manifest input on both the win32 shell:true path and the POSIX execFile path
